annotations.text: Although this patient had proteinuria, it was not severe enough to warrant a diagnosis of preeclampsia; in addition, the onset of preeclampsia would be unlikely this early in the pregnancy
the presence of hypokalemia increased my suspicion that the problem was secondary hypertension

The vast majority of patients who receive a diagnosis of gestational diabetes have a relatively mild degree of carbohydrate intolerance that develops late in pregnancy and is associated with the insulin resistance of pregnancy
The glycated hemoglobin value of 8.2% at 16 weeks and 6 days' gestation led me to suspect that she had had hyperglycemia for some time before her pregnancy began
Low-dose dexamethasone suppression testing can be used to diagnose Cushing's syndrome despite the occurrence of false negative results
Although a pituitary tumor is the most common cause of Cushing's syndrome, an adrenal source accounts for 40 to 50% of the cases in pregnant patients
The single finding that appropriately prompted an evaluation of this patient for Cushing's syndrome was unexplained hypokalemia
The normal aldosterone level and elevated plasma renin activity were consistent with pregnancy, ruling out an aldosterone-secreting tumor as a cause of hypokalemia
the corticotropin level of 3 pg per milliliter (0.6 nmol per liter) in the setting of profound hypercortisolemia was strongly suggestive of an adrenal source of Cushing's syndrome
rapidly escalating hypertension and insulin-requiring diabetes made surgical cure an immediate goal
prompt adrenalectomy, as compared with postponement of the surgery until after delivery, reduces the risk of maternal complications, fetal death, premature birth, and neonatal complications
This tumor was larger than the median size of an adenoma and had focal hemorrhage but had no gross features of a carcinom
Microscopical examination of adrenal cortical carcinomas usually shows atypical mitotic cells, vascular and capsular invasion, and extension into adjacent adipose tissue
Although it is clinically important to distinguish functioning from nonfunctioning adrenal tumors, it is usually not possible to make this distinction on the basis of the pathological findings
the presence of a tumor in the liver is diagnostic of adrenal cortical carcinoma
A recent gene expression profile study identified 91 genes, including the  *IGF2* gene, that are associated with adrenal cortical carcinom
